title: Strengthen guarded-group test assertions in LSP
analysis:
Task 437 introduced guarded-group tests in two files but left assertions weak:

1. **analysis.rs (lines 429-488)**: Tests `member_names_includes_guarded_group_members` and `member_names_includes_else_block_members` use `member_names()` which returns `(name, ValueCellKind, &Type)`. They assert kind and type but NOT span or cell_type from MemberInfo. Since `member_names()` doesn't return span info, we supplement each test with `find_member_decl()` calls (which returns `MemberInfo` with `.span`, `.cell_type`, `.kind`, `.name`) to verify that guarded-group members have correct span coverage and cell_type consistency. This follows the pattern established by existing tests like `find_member_decl_width_is_param` (line 359) which asserts span start, decl_text substring, and cell_type.

2. **goto_def.rs (lines 150-182)**: Tests `goto_def_param_inside_where_block` and `goto_def_let_inside_else_block` assert `range.start.line` and `range.start.character` but never assert `range.end` positions. Adding `range.end.line` and `range.end.character` assertions to the guarded-group tests strengthens regression coverage — a bug in span end computation for nested members would go undetected without these.

All changes are test-only. No production code modifications needed. Assertions should pass against current code since find_member_decl and span_to_range are already correct (verified by other existing tests). If any assertion fails, it reveals a latent bug requiring a fix.

steps.description: Strengthen `member_names_includes_guarded_group_members` (analysis.rs ~line 429): After the existing member_names() tuple assertions, add a `ctx.find_member_decl("guarded_x", None)` call and assert: (a) info.span.start < info.span.end, (b) `&source[info.span.start as usize..info.span.end as usize]` contains "guarded_x" and "5mm" (following the pattern from find_member_decl_width_is_param at line 366), (c) `*info.cell_type == Type::length()`, (d) `info.kind == ValueCellKind::Param`. This verifies span and cell_type for a where-block member via the MemberInfo API.
Run `cargo test -p reify-lsp member_names_includes_guarded_group_members` to verify step-1 assertions pass. If any fail, diagnose the root cause in find_member_decl or find_named_member_span and fix.

Strengthen `member_names_includes_else_block_members` (analysis.rs ~line 455): After the existing member_names() tuple assertions, add `ctx.find_member_decl()` calls for both "when_true" and "when_false". Assert for each: (a) span text via `&source[span.start as usize..span.end as usize]` contains the member name and its default value ("1mm" for when_true, "2mm" for when_false), (b) `*info.cell_type == Type::length()`, (c) `info.kind == ValueCellKind::Param`. This ensures else-branch span resolution is correct.
Run `cargo test -p reify-lsp member_names_includes_else_block_members` to verify step-3 assertions pass. If any fail, diagnose and fix.

Strengthen `goto_def_param_inside_where_block` (goto_def.rs ~line 150): Add `assert_eq!(loc.range.end.line, 3)` and `assert_eq!(loc.range.end.character, <computed>)`. The declaration "        param guarded_x : Scalar = 5mm" on line 3 has 8-space indent + 31 chars = end character 39. Compute the exact value by counting the source string to handle any off-by-one. Add descriptive assertion messages.

Run `cargo test -p reify-lsp goto_def_param_inside_where_block` to verify step-5 assertions pass. If end character is wrong, adjust the expected value based on actual span_to_range output (tree-sitter span boundaries may differ from manual count).

Strengthen `goto_def_let_inside_else_block` (goto_def.rs ~line 167): Add `assert_eq!(loc.range.end.line, 5)` and `assert_eq!(loc.range.end.character, <computed>)`. The declaration "        let fallback = 10" on line 5 has 8-space indent + 18 chars = end character 26. Compute the exact value by counting the source string. Add descriptive assertion messages.
Run `cargo test -p reify-lsp goto_def_let_inside_else_block` to verify step-7 assertions pass. Adjust expected end character if needed.
Run full LSP crate test suite `cargo test -p reify-lsp` to confirm no regressions. All existing tests plus the strengthened assertions must pass.

Rebase task/596 onto current main (8f936e96). Main advanced with 2 commits after the previous partial merge: a task-status chore commit and `feat(stdlib): add Physical, Rigid, Flexible structural traits`. Our 2 remaining commits (e2a1eb6b goto_def range.end assertions, 5c16219c plan chore) should rebase cleanly since neither new main commit touches goto_def.rs. Run `git rebase main` and resolve any conflicts if they arise.
Run `cargo test -p reify-lsp` after rebase to verify all LSP tests still pass against the rebased code. The new main commits (stdlib structural traits) should not affect LSP test behavior.

design_decisions.decision: Supplement member_names tests with find_member_decl calls rather than changing member_names() return type
design_decisions.rationale: member_names() is a public API returning (name, kind, type) tuples. Changing its signature would break callers. Instead, we add find_member_decl() calls within the same test to verify span and cell_type, following the pattern already established in the codebase (find_member_decl_width_is_param at line 359). This validates the same data through a different code path, increasing coverage.

design_decisions.decision: Assert span text content rather than exact byte offsets
design_decisions.rationale: The existing pattern (e.g., find_member_decl_width_is_param at line 366) asserts that source[span.start..span.end] contains expected substrings rather than hardcoding byte positions. This is more resilient to whitespace or formatting changes while still validating span correctness.

design_decisions.decision: Add range.end assertions to goto_def tests using computed character offsets
design_decisions.rationale: goto_def returns an LSP Location with a Range (start + end positions). Existing guarded-group tests only assert start position. Adding end position assertions catches regressions where span_to_range produces incorrect end offsets for nested declarations. No other goto_def test currently asserts end range either, but the guarded-group tests are the highest-risk since their spans traverse GuardedGroup recursion in find_named_member_span.

design_decisions.decision: Rebase rather than merge to resolve main advancement
design_decisions.rationale: The merge_ff_failed review blocker indicates main advanced during the merge window. Rebasing our 2 remaining commits onto current main produces a clean linear history suitable for fast-forward merge. No conflicts expected since the advancing commits (stdlib structural traits, task-status chore) don't touch goto_def.rs.

reuse.how: Call find_member_decl(name, None) to get MemberInfo with span, cell_type, kind fields — supplements the (name, kind, type) tuple from member_names()

reuse.what: Span text extraction pattern from find_member_decl_width_is_param
reuse.how: Use `let decl_text = &source[info.span.start as usize..info.span.end as usize]; assert!(decl_text.contains(...))` to verify span covers the full declaration text

reuse.what: test_uri() helper and AnalysisContext::new() already in test modules
reuse.how: Both test modules already have these — no new helpers or imports needed
